entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The main crop studied, Arachis hypogaea L., for economic viability and productivity.

entities.name: ECONOMIC VIABILITY
entities.description: The measure of financial success and productivity in groundnut cultivation.

entities.name: RECOMMENDED DOSE OF FERTILIZER
entities.description: Application of 25:50:25 NPK and Gypsum at 500 kg ha-1.

entities.name: MICRO NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Application of ZnSO4 at 25 kg ha-1 and Borax at 10 kg ha-1.

entities.name: BROAD BED AND FURROW
entities.description: A cultivation bedding technique found superior to flat bed.

entities.name: ICGV 91114
entities.description: A specific variety of groundnut that showed superior economic performance.

entities.name: K 6
entities.description: A specific variety of groundnut used for comparison.

entities.name: FARMER'S PRACTICE
entities.description: Traditional cultivation methods used by farmers, which resulted in the lowest economic value.

entities.name: HIREGUNDGAL
entities.description: The specific site where the experiment was carried out.

entities.name: TUMKUR
entities.description: The district in Karnataka where the experiment was conducted.

entities.name: KARNATAKA
entities.description: The state in India where the experiment was conducted.

entities.name: KHARIF SEASON
entities.description: The specific agricultural season of 2016 during which the experiment took place.

relationships.description: The study evaluates the economic viability of groundnut cultivation
Proved superior in terms of economic viability
Contributed to superior economic viability when added to fertilizer
Found to be superior over flat bed for economics
Found to be superior to variety K 6 in economics
Registered the lowest value for economics
